title: Market Brief — Friday, March 27, 2026
summary: LLMflation hit 21.41, meaning the cost of frontier-grade intelligence has collapsed 79% from GPT-4 launch baseline in three years. DeepSeek V3.2 output at $0.38/M tokens is now 158x cheaper than o3 Pro at $80/M, and the floor is still falling.
paragraphs: LLMflation hit 21.41, meaning the cost of frontier-grade intelligence has collapsed 79% from GPT-4 launch baseline in three years. DeepSeek V3.2 output at $0.38/M tokens is now 158x cheaper than o3 Pro at $80/M, and the floor is still falling.

B200 inference margins are razor-thin: medium-tier providers selling Llama 70B lose 90 cents on every dollar at $3/M output, and even high-tier barely breaks even at 5% margin. DeepSeek is selling R1 output at $2.50/M against estimated costs north of $4/M, meaning DeepSeek is selling at a loss of roughly $1.50 per million tokens to buy market share.

o3 Pro at $80/M output is overvalued when GPT-5.4 delivers comparable reasoning at $15/M, an 81% discount from the same provider. DeepSeek V3.2 and Llama 4 Maverick at sub-$0.60/M output are undervalued commodity plays; accumulate GPU capacity on H100 spot while it dips and route non-critical inference to open-weight models.
bullets: $0.38 per million output tokens is DeepSeek V3.2's price, the lowest of all 18 tracked models.
21.41 is the LLMflation index, down from 100 at GPT-4 launch just three years ago.
$1.47 per hour is H100 spot median, down 4.3% overnight signaling softening demand.
18% availability on H200s is the tightest GPU class, with only 63 of 350 units open.
$80 per million output tokens makes o3 Pro 133x more expensive than Grok 4.1 Fast.
90% margin at premium tier on H100 confirms frontier API markup still dwarfs commodity hardware cost.
